title: Corte Suprema di Cassazione (Supreme Court of Cassation), 5th criminal section, 2728/2023 UP – 12/10/2023
text:
The fact occurred o n 30 th July 2018, on a vessel – the Asso 28 -  f lying the Italian flag and owned by the shipping company 'Augusta off shore ' of Naples. The vessel was in international waters, acting as a supply vessel for the Sabratha oil platform, owned by Mellitah Oil & Gas, a company in which Eni North Africa and the Libyan National Oil Corporation (NOC) have an interest. The defendant , Master of the ASSO 28, was on board the vessel, in proximity of the above-mentioned platform, about fifty-seven nautical miles off the Libyan coast, in international waters and in the Libyan Search and Rescue (SAR) zone .   

In these circumstances, the defendant detected a n inflatable boat with 101 migrants on board in distress conditions and proceeded to embark the shipwrecked migrants on his vessel.     

The M aster rescued and took on board 101 migrants , including pregnant women (5) and minors (5) . Consequently, acting in agreement with the staff of the platform, he embarked an alleged ‘ Libyan customs officer ’ to the Asso28, without performing any identification of the individual’s qualification or credentials.   

The defendant did not notify immediately the competent coordination and rescue centre , namely the Tripoli Coordination and Rescue Centre n or the Maritime Rescue Coordination Centre in Rome – as he would have been required to do in case of failing to receive an answer to the former centre . H e did not activate the coordination of the competent SAR authorities nor compl ied with the information obligations under Article 5 of Resolution MSC 167/168 (concerning the relief vessel, the survivors, the actions taken and to be taken and the decisions regarding asylum seekers) . T he defendant failed to identify the migrants, to obtain information on their origin and nationality, on their state of health, to subject them to a medical examination, to ascertain whether they wished to apply for asylum, and to ascertain whether the minors were alone or accompanied .   

The defendant set course for the Libyan coast and , once in Libyan territorial waters, transferred the 101 shipwrecked migrants onto a Libyan patrol boat .
